perturbation.original_text: "Controlled Technology" means any software, documentation, technology, or other technical data, or any products that include or use any of the foregoing, of which the export, re-export, or release to certain jurisdictions or countries is prohibited or requires an export license or other governmental approval under any Law, including the U.S. Export Administration Act and its associated regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: "Controlled Technology" means any software, documentation, technology, or other technical data, or any products that include or use any of the foregoing, of which the export, re-export, or release to certain jurisdictions or countries requires an export license or other governmental approval under any Law.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Controlled Technology' includes software, documentation, or technical data whose export, re-export, or release is 'prohibited' or 'requires an export license'. The removal of 'is prohibited' introduces an ambiguity because the edited definition no longer explicitly covers scenarios where export is completely forbidden, potentially leading to disputes about whether certain technologies are subject to the agreement's export restrictions. This is contradicted in Section 14.5 where there is an obligation for both parties not to export controlled technology that is prohibited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party will comply with all applicable Laws and the Operating Regulations, governmental requirements, and industry standards, including those with respect to privacy, data protection, portability, or accountability, applicable to such Party or its personnel with respect to the Software, the Services, and the performance of its obligations under this Agreement; provided that Allscripts will have no obligation to comply with any Operating Regulations unless such Operating Regulations are disclosed to it. Neither Party will, nor permit any third parties to, export, re-export, or release, directly or indirectly, any Controlled Technology to any country or jurisdiction to which the export, re-export, or release of any Controlled Technology (a) is prohibited by applicable Law or (b) without first completing all required undertakings (including obtaining any necessary export license or other governmental approval).
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Controlled Technology' included explicit prohibitions ('is prohibited') alongside the requirement for licenses. The changed text removes the prohibition, narrowing the scope to only technologies requiring licenses. This directly contradicts the later section stating, 'Neither Party will... export... any Controlled Technology to any country... to which the export... of any Controlled Technology (a) is prohibited by applicable Law.' The removal of 'is prohibited' from the definition of Controlled Technology creates an inconsistency with the clause that prohibits the export of controlled technology when it is prohibited by law because it is unclear whether a technology whose export is prohibited is considered 'Controlled Technology' under the modified definition. This makes the later clause unenforceable with respect to such technologies.

perturbation.original_text: The Company has delivered or made available to Allscripts complete and accurate Documentation for the Installed Software, Subscription Software Services and that required to offer the Merchant Processing Services, and will promptly deliver or make available to Allscripts supplements to such Documentation and manuals, as and when released, to reflect all modifications, releases, supplements, corrections, Updates, amendments, and other changes to the Installed Software or Subscription Software Services or that required to offer the Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company has delivered or made available to Allscripts Documentation for the Installed Software, Subscription Software Services and that required to offer the Merchant Processing Services, and will promptly deliver or make available to Allscripts supplements to such Documentation and manuals, as and when released, to reflect all modifications, releases, supplements, corrections, Updates, amendments, and other changes to the Installed Software or Subscription Software Services or that required to offer the Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.explanation: The deletion of 'complete and accurate' from the documentation clause diminishes the guaranteed quality of the Documentation provided. This undermines the assurance of thoroughness and precision originally promised, potentially conflicting with the performance warranty detailed in Section 21.3(a). The contradiction lies in that section 21.3(a) requires there to be applicable specifications in the agreement as well as the Documentation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Company represents, warrants, and covenants to Allscripts that, during the term of this Agreement and any Customer Agreement: (a) when used in accordance with the Documentation, all Subscription Software Services or Merchant Processing Services and the Installed Software as provided by the Company will meet, in all material respects, all applicable specifications set forth in this Agreement and the Documentation, and function in all material respects, in conformity with this Agreement and the Documentation; (b) any media on which the Installed Software or Documentation is delivered will be free of any damage or defect in design, material or workmanship; and (c) no Update will have a material adverse effect on the material functionality or operability of the Installed Software or Subscription Software Services or Merchant Processing Services, as the case may be.
perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees 'complete and accurate' Documentation. The changed text removes this guarantee, directly contradicting the requirement in the Contradicted Text (Section 21.3(a)) that the Subscription Software Services or Merchant Processing Services and the Installed Software will meet all applicable specifications set forth in this Agreement and the Documentation when used in accordance with the Documentation. By removing 'complete and accurate,' the Company no longer explicitly warrants the Documentation will be sufficient to meet the specifications, potentially rendering the warranty in Section 21.3(a) unenforceable or ambiguous, as compliance with the Documentation may not ensure the services meet the specified requirements.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party will pay its own costs and expenses (including counsel fees) of any arbitration; provided, however, that the Parties will equally share the fees and expenses of the arbitrators; provided, further, that in the event any action, suit, arbitration, or other proceeding is instituted or commenced by either Party against the other Party arising hereunder, the prevailing Party will be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys' fees, court costs, and costs of arbitration from the non-prevailing Party (it being agreed that the arbitrators and/or judge may eliminate or reduce such recovery on the grounds that it is unreasonable or disproportionate to the harm suffered).
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will pay its own costs and expenses (including counsel fees) of any arbitration; provided, however, that the Parties will equally share the fees and expenses of the arbitrators.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the 'prevailing Party' clause from the arbitration section, it generates an internal contradiction because it removes the incentive to win such cases since there is no more reimbursement for lawyer costs etc. This contradicts the need for dispute resolution set in section 28.9(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Except as expressly permitted in Section 28.9(f), neither Party will initiate an arbitration of any dispute hereunder unless (i) such Party has provided the other Party with written notice of that dispute with reasonable specificity and attempted in good faith to resolve that dispute through negotiations; (ii) despite such efforts, the dispute remains unresolved [***] after receipt of that notice; and (iii) such initiation is in accordance with this Section 28.9.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the 'prevailing Party' clause in the Changed Text directly contradicts the purpose of Section 28.9(a) in the Contradicted Text. Section 28.9(a) outlines the process for initiating arbitration, including a good faith effort to resolve disputes through negotiation. By removing the provision for the prevailing party to recover attorney's fees, the Changed Text diminishes the incentive for parties to engage in arbitration and effectively undermines the dispute resolution mechanism established in Section 28.9(a). This creates a significant inconsistency regarding the intended enforcement and resolution of disputes under the contract.
